steps.title: Identify the scale factor from corresponding sides
steps.content: Triangle ABC and triangle DEF are similar, with AB and DE as corresponding sides. Since $DE = 2 \cdot AB$, the linear scale factor is $k = 2$.

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to the perimeter
steps.content:
For similar figures, perimeters scale by the same factor as the sides. Since each side of triangle DEF is 2 times the corresponding side in triangle ABC:
$$\text{Perimeter of } \triangle DEF = 2 \times 20 = 40$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
